finding.summary: Significant left-right level imbalance and overly bass-heavy tonal balance.
finding.probable_cause: The left channel is 4.1 dB louder than the right (balance_db: -4.1). The spectrum shows -18 dB at 100 Hz, -24 dB at 1 kHz, -30 dB at 4 kHz, indicating a strong low-frequency emphasis and weak highs, which may sound muddy.
finding.confidence_reason: Balance_db is a direct measurement; silence fraction is low (0.05) so measurement is reliable. Spectrum has only three bands; full picture unclear but indicates substantial roll-off and possible lack of clarity.

evidence_refs.description: Only three bands: 100 Hz at -18 dB, 1 kHz at -24 dB, 4 kHz at -30 dB

proposal.reason: Compensate for the left-heavy imbalance. Adjusting pan to the right will reduce left channel level relative to right, bringing the stereo balance closer to zero.
